content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN bags and an integer XXX. The ithi^{th}ith bag contains AiA_iAiвАЛ coins such that AiвЙ§XA_i \leq XAiвАЛвЙ§X.

In one operation, Chef can:


Pick any bag and increase its coins to XXX. Formally, if he choses the ithi^{th}ith bag, he can set Ai=XA_i = XAiвАЛ=X.
Given that the cost of performing **each** operation is CCC (CвЙ§X)(C \leq X)(CвЙ§X) coins and Chef can perform the above operation any (possibly zero) number of times, determine the **maximum** value of

(вИСi=1NAi)(\sum_{i=1}^N A_i)(вИСi=1NвАЛAiвАЛ) вИТ-вИТ (total cost paid by Chef),

if Chef performs the operations optimally.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, XXX, and CCC вАФ the number of bags, maximum limit of coins on each bag and cost of each operation respectively.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,вА¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1вАЛ,A2вАЛ,вА¶,ANвАЛ - denoting the number of coins in each bag.




Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum value of вИСi=1NAi\sum_{i=1}^N A_iвИСi=1NвАЛAiвАЛ вИТ-вИТ total cost paid by Chef.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It is optimal for Chef to perform 222 operations:

Operation 111: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and set A1=5A_1 = 5A1вАЛ=5 by using 222 coins.
Operation 222: Choose i=2i = 2i=2 and set A2=5A_2 = 5A2вАЛ=5 by using 222 coins.

The final array is A=[5,5,3,4,5]A = [5, 5, 3, 4, 5]A=[5,5,3,4,5] and the total cost is 2+2=42+2 = 42+2=4. Thus, the value of вИСi=1NAi\sum_{i=1}^N A_iвИСi=1NвАЛAiвАЛ вИТ-вИТ total cost is (5+5+3+4+5)вИТ4=22вИТ4=18(5+5+3+4+5) - 4 = 22-4 = 18(5+5+3+4+5)вИТ4=22вИТ4=18.
Test case 222: It is optimal for Chef to perform 000 operations. Thus, the final array remains [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1] and the cost is 000. The value of вИСi=1NAi\sum_{i=1}^N A_iвИСi=1NвАЛAiвАЛ вИТ-вИТ total cost is (1+1+1)вИТ0=3(1+1+1) - 0 = 3(1+1+1)вИТ0=3.
Test case 333: It is optimal for Chef to perform 000 operations. Thus, the final array remains [3,2,3,1,1][3, 2, 3, 1, 1][3,2,3,1,1] and the cost is 000. The value of вИСi=1NAi\sum_{i=1}^N A_iвИСi=1NвАЛAiвАЛ вИТ-вИТ total cost is (3+2+3+1+1)вИТ0=10(3+2+3+1+1) - 0 = 10(3+2+3+1+1)вИТ0=10.
